<par>These two Nanhua forms, <latinform>phe²¹me̱³³</latinform> and <latinform>tʰᴀ̱²¹me̱³³</latinform>, are perhaps evidence of dialect mixture. The PLB cluster <latinform>*pl-</latinform> sometimes loses the lateral element, but sometimes develops into a retroflex stop (as in Mojiang and Dafang). It is possible that the Nanhua form with retroflex initial is borrowed from such a dialect.</par>
<par>Cf. Lyuzu <latinform>nbo³³ly⁵³</latinform> 'kidney'. Both <latinform>lu̵⁵³</latinform> and <latinform>ly⁵³</latinform> are probably < <reconstruction>*lum</reconstruction>.</par>
<par>The third morpheme in this form, <latinform>tsi³³</latinform>, seems to be a widespread Guiqiong noun suffix, probably borrowed from Chinese <hanform>子</hanform>.</par>

<par>The first syllable of WT <latinform>sgo-pur</latinform> 'foreskin (vulg.)' does not appear to descend from this etymon, but seems rather to mean 'door; aperture, outlet'. However, Jäschke does not include this under compounds with <latinform>sgo</latinform> 'door' (114-6), but rather lists it as a separate head-entry (p. 116). The meaning of the second syllable <latinform>-pur</latinform> is unclear, though there might be a connection with <latinform>ḥp'ur-ba</latinform> 'wrap up, envelop'.</par>
